title: On-Call Guide
bodyMarkdown:
What to do when you are the Helios on-call.

## Rotation
Weekly rotation across the SRE-facing engineers. Handoff happens Monday morning with a short sync on anything still open from the prior week.

## Primary responsibilities
Respond to pages within fifteen minutes during business hours, thirty minutes off hours. Triage first: is this a known failure mode with a runbook, or something new. Most pages map to an existing runbook, check the checkpoint restore runbook and the NFS mount troubleshooting page first for anything storage-shaped, and the serving rollback runbook for anything deploy-shaped.

## Escalation
If you cannot resolve within thirty minutes, pull in the relevant owner: Priya Natarajan for storage, Owen Reyes for serving, Jonah Kim for anything compiler-adjacent. Do not sit on a page alone past that window.

## Incident hygiene
Open a JIRA incident for anything customer-facing, even if resolved quickly. Future on-calls rely on that history; several existing runbooks trace back to a single incident writeup.

## After your week
Hand off any open threads explicitly, do not assume the next on-call will find them. Elena Petrova maintains this guide.
